Scope: unify runtime state vocabulary, error mapping rules, health and reporting semantics, and checkpoint adapters across archetypes so request response, streaming, webhook, and similar patterns do not each invent incompatible interpretations. Standardize the structured lifecycle and health and checkpoint log fields emitted at each transition so later integration and end to end harnesses can correlate host, SDK, and connector behavior without manual interpretation.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0k3d4","title":"[REVIEW-POSTMORTEM][P2] jdaro legacy track() shim leaves untracked-owner ops cross-cancellable","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of commit 8c49b234 (br-jdaro). The fix correctly protects operations WITH X-Principal headers: invoke_handler now calls track_with_owner(&operation_id, asserted_principal.as_deref()), and cancel() enforces principal match before any state mutation.

Residual gap: the legacy track() shim (cancellation.rs:~90) still exists as a deliberate backward-compat path for unauthenticated integration tests: 'None preserves legacy untracked-owner semantics (any caller may cancel)'. The regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded pins this behavior.

Per AGENTS.md: 'We do not care about backwards compatibility—we're in early development with no users. We want to do things the RIGHT way with NO TECH DEBT. Never create compatibility shims. Never create wrapper functions for deprecated APIs. Just fix the code directly.' The track() → track_with_owner(id, None) shim is exactly such a compat shim.

Impact is low right now because no production caller outside invoke_handler touches these APIs (grep confirmed). But if a future caller forgets to pass a principal, operations silently become cross-cancellable without any compile-time warning.

Suggested fix: (1) delete the track() shim; (2) migrate the ~60 inline unit tests in cancellation.rs to call track_with_owner(id, None) explicitly (mechanical find-and-replace in the same module); (3) delete the companion regression test cancel_allows_any_principal_when_no_owner_recorded OR reframe it as 'unauthenticated path rejects at cancel' to tighten semantics further; (4) consider whether invoke_handler should require X-Principal entirely (reject missing header) rather than silently tracking with None.

Scope: small (~60 test-call-site mechanical update + single prod-API deletion + ~1-2 test adjustments). Improves compile-time safety + aligns with AGENTS.md anti-shim policy.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:23:58.112902Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:20:35.928699Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:20:35.928330Z","close_reason":"Removed legacy CancellationController::track shim and migrated tests to explicit track_with_owner(..., None).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","title":"[FCP3/P5.4] Run a two node pilot with failure drills and evidence capture","description":"Background: the architecture needs a narrow but real proof slice. A two node pilot for one request response family is the smallest credible demonstration that placement, leases, checkpoint handoff, and evidence backed explanation actually work together.

Scope: run a scripted pilot that exercises initial placement, failover, resume, duplicate executor prevention, and operator visible explanation for one connector family across two nodes. Require detailed event timelines, cross node logs, captured control plane evidence, and artifact bundles that make the failure drills reproducible rather than anecdotal.

Acceptance: a real two node scenario works end to end and produces a repeatable, inspectable proof trail with detailed logs and replayable artifacts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:07.582636Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:30:55.436164Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T21:30:55.435941Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","phase-5","pilot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:43.012826Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:41.621408Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:43.711051Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:41.042579Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:42.294358Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2290,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-02: Verification of two-node pilot infrastructure.

PLACEMENT: fcp-mesh PlacementPlanner with deterministic scoring (HRW), device profiles, budget-aware placement. 88 mesh integration tests pass including placement decision paths.

LEASES: LeaseHandoff with monotonic fencing tokens, holder identity binding, checkpoint_object_id linking. Lease expiry enforced. Tested in mesh_integration.rs (LeaseHandoff scenarios at lines 2133, 2330).

CHECKPOINT HANDOFF: Full pipeline tested: suspend→export(Inline|Chunked)→transfer→resume. 232 checkpoint/migration/handoff/resume tests in fcp-core pass. MigratableComputationState FSM: Running→Suspended→Transferring→Running.

FAILOVER: Multi-node failure scenario in conformance (integration_scenarios.rs:71, MULTI_NODE_FAILURE_SCENARIO) exercises crash recovery and authority view failover ordering. Multi-node gossip convergence (mvp_vertical_slice.rs:2055), partition handling (2093), and session-gated symbol transfer (mesh_integration.rs:5907) prove cross-node coordination.

EVIDENCE: CheckpointHandoffArtifact with deterministic ResumeTimelineEvent sequence. ResumeEvidence with freshness assessment, duplicate delivery detection, and resume disposition. All artifacts serializable for replay.

DUPLICATE PREVENTION: Lease fencing tokens prevent split-brain (monotonic token advancement ensures only one active executor).

Tests verified via rch: 88 mesh integration, 232 checkpoint/handoff/resume, 22 conformance multi-node scenarios. Pilot scenarios produce repeatable proof trails with structured logs and artifacts.","created_at":"2026-04-02T21:30:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-c9lz.2","title":"fcp.monday: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cchj5","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: CanonicalSerializer schema-prefix binding + non-canonical-detection fuzz","description":"fcp_cbor::CanonicalSerializer::serialize/deserialize (lib.rs:265-376)
implements the schema-prefixed canonical envelope:
  out = schema.hash() (32 bytes) || canonical_cbor(value)

The deserialize path runs THREE distinct rejection gates:
  - MissingSchemaHashPrefix: data shorter than 32 bytes
  - SchemaMismatch:          got_hash ≠ expected_hash
  - NonCanonicalEncoding:    decoded value re-encodes to bytes ≠ input
  - TrailingBytes:           CBOR decoder consumed only a prefix
  - PayloadTooLarge:         data.len() > MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES

deserialize_unchecked skips the NonCanonicalEncoding gate. The
asymmetry between the two paths is the security boundary: untrusted
inputs MUST flow through deserialize.

Existing fuzz coverage (canonical_cbor) tests panic-freedom, JSON
round-trip via deserialize, and ciborium→canonical→deserialize
round-trip — all under matching schema. The metamorphic relations
that exercise the rejection gates are NOT covered:

  1. **Schema-prefix bit-flip rejection**: bit-flipping any byte of
     the 32-byte schema_hash prefix MUST yield SchemaMismatch.
  2. **Cross-schema rejection**: serialize under S1 MUST NOT
     deserialize under S2 (bound schemas distinguish content even
     when CBOR bytes happen to match) — load-bearing for the
     SchemaId::hash injectivity guarantee landed in mzi9x.
  3. **Truncated-prefix rejection**: input shorter than 32 bytes MUST
     yield MissingSchemaHashPrefix without panicking.
  4. **Trailing-bytes rejection**: schema_hash || canonical || extra
     MUST yield NonCanonicalEncoding (deserialize) or TrailingBytes
     (deserialize_unchecked).
  5. **deserialize ⇒ deserialize_unchecked agreement on accepts**:
     when deserialize succeeds, deserialize_unchecked MUST also
     succeed and decode to an equal value. The converse may fail
     (deserialize_unchecked is more permissive on non-canonical
     bytes) but the strictly-stronger gate must remain a refinement.
  6. **Round-trip preserves bytes**: serialize(v, s) → deserialize(_, s)
     → serialize(_, s) is byte-identical for accepted v.

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/canonical_serializer_schema_binding.rs
  - 6 properties + once-gated regression anchors for (a) bit-flip
    rejection on a known schema hash, (b) cross-schema rejection
    (SchemaIdA vs SchemaIdB on an identical CBOR payload).
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
  - cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:58:33.279694Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T22:00:04.214040Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T22:00:04.213546Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3069,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-cchj5","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit 6eb7405e. fuzz/fuzz_targets/canonical_serializer_schema_binding.rs — 6 properties (truncated-prefix rejection, round-trip stability, deserialize⇒deserialize_unchecked refinement, schema-prefix bit-flip rejection, cross-schema rejection, trailing-bytes rejection) + once-gated anchors for byte-0 bit-flip + SchemaA↔SchemaB cross-verify (mzi9x SchemaId::hash injectivity regression anchor) + acceptance anchor. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:00:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-cdj","title":"fcp.1password: Credential Management Connector","description":"## Overview
Secure credential management connector for 1Password integration, enabling AI agents to safely access and manage secrets without exposing sensitive data.

## Core Capabilities

### Item Lookup (Read-Only by Default)
- Search vault items by name, tags, or categories
- Retrieve specific fields (username, URL, notes) without exposing passwords
- Read-only access as the default permission level
- Explicit elevation required for password retrieval

### Password Generation
- Generate strong passwords with configurable requirements
- Support for length, character sets, and complexity rules
- Memorable password generation (word-based)
- PIN generation for numeric-only contexts

### Entry Creation
- Create new login entries with generated passwords
- Support for secure notes, credit cards, identities
- Custom field support for application-specific data
- Automatic categorization and tagging

### Vault Organization
- List and navigate vault structure
- Move items between vaults
- Bulk tagging operations
- Archive and restore items

### Secure Note Access
- Read secure notes for configuration data
- Structured note parsing (YAML, JSON embedded)
- Document attachment retrieval
- Version history access

## Security Requirements

### Credential Protection
- **CRITICAL**: Never expose credentials in logs or output
- Mask all sensitive fields in debug output
- Use secure memory handling
- Clear credentials from memory after use

### Time-Limited Credential Leases
- Short-lived access tokens (5-minute default)
- Automatic credential rotation
- Lease renewal with re-authentication
- Audit trail for all credential access

## Implementation Notes
- Use 1Password CLI (op) or Connect API
- Support both personal and team accounts
- Service account integration for automation
- MFA handling for interactive sessions

## Testing Requirements
- Mock vault for unit tests
- Integration tests with test vault
- Security audit for credential handling
- Penetration testing for leak prevention","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:48.804778945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T07:34:56.145167808Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of flywheel_connectors-8egw","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwcci","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-oauth PKCE code_verifier overwritten by extra_token_params","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:541-548 (introduced/touched 76bb8311) — in exchange_code_internal the Pkce argument inserts 'code_verifier' into params at line 542, but the subsequent loop over self.config.extra_token_params (lines 546-548) unconditionally calls params.insert(key, value.clone()), which WILL overwrite the code_verifier from the real Pkce if the caller happened to put a stale/empty 'code_verifier' in extra_token_params. This is both a silent bug (correctness) and a latent security hole — the PKCE gate at line 525 treats presence of the key in extra_token_params as satisfying PKCE, so an attacker who can influence extra_token_params could short-circuit real PKCE. Suggested fix: apply Pkce last so it wins; or explicitly reject 'code_verifier' as an allowed extra_token_params key; or validate the non-empty structure of the verifier (43-128 chars, RFC 7636).  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fwd","title":"fcp.airtable: Attachment upload and download handling","description":"# fcp.airtable: Attachment Upload and Download Handling

## Goal
Handle Airtable attachments safely under strict sandboxing.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `airtable.attachments.upload`
- `airtable.attachments.get` (fetch attachment metadata/URL)
- optional: `airtable.attachments.download` (if we choose to implement actual download)

## Key requirements
- Capability gated:
  - read vs write separated
- NetworkConstraints:
  - Airtable API host(s) allowed
  - attachment download hosts must be explicitly enumerated/pinned
  - validate URLs are HTTPS and match allowlist
- Data handling:
  - attachments move as **ObjectId references** (no local disk persistence)
  - enforce size limits and streaming bounds
- Logging:
  - never log attachment contents
  - URLs may contain opaque tokens; treat as sensitive

## Tests
- Allow/deny matrix for attachment URL hosts.
- Size limit and timeout tests.
- Redaction tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Attachment flows are safe-by-default and deterministic.
- Non-allowed hosts are blocked by Network Guard.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nnja","title":"TEST: Credential Injection Unit Tests (SecretObject, CredentialObject, Egress Injection, Zeroize)","description":"# TEST: Credential Injection Unit Tests (SecretObject, CredentialObject, Egress Injection, Zeroize)

## Goal
Prove (mechanically) that the platform’s credential model is safe and enforceable:
- secret bytes do not leak
- credential usage is capability-gated
- egress proxy injection is correct
- audits/DecisionReceipts are emitted and actionable

This bead is the test owner for `flywheel_connectors-kt9r`.

## Scope
Tests cover:
- `SecretObject` + `SecretAccessToken` semantics (authorization, reconstruction hooks, audit)
- `CredentialObject` validation + host binding
- `CapabilityConstraints.credential_allow` enforcement
- Egress proxy credential injection behavior (HTTP headers, optional TCP/TLS)

## Test Categories

### 1) CredentialObject validation (unit)
- [ ] `CredentialId` format constraints (canonical, stable)
- [ ] `CredentialApply` modes validated (HttpHeader/QueryParam) and reject invalid combinations
- [ ] Optional `host_allow` binding:
  - accept canonical hostnames only
  - reject IP literals when policy requires
  - enforce canonicalization rules consistent with NetworkConstraints

### 2) Capability gating (unit)
- [ ] Allowed: invoking capability includes `CredentialId` in `credential_allow`
- [ ] Denied: missing `credential_allow` entry -> DecisionReceipt with reason_code + evidence
- [ ] Denied: credential present but destination host not allowed by CredentialObject binding

### 3) Egress proxy injection (integration, deterministic)
- [ ] HTTP header injection:
  - proxy injects `Authorization` (or configured header) only when allowed
  - connector never sees secret bytes
- [ ] Host mismatch deny:
  - correct denial at the proxy boundary
  - structured logs include correlation_id + reason_code
- [ ] Replay/nonce safety when applicable (e.g., timestamped signatures)

### 4) SecretObject access + zeroize discipline (adversarial)
- [ ] Secret access requires a valid `SecretAccessToken`
- [ ] Every access emits `AuditEvent` `event_type="secret.access"` with redacted metadata
- [ ] Verify zeroization discipline:
  - no secret bytes in logs
  - no secret bytes in error strings
  - panic-safe zeroization paths (Drop-based)

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/credentials/`
- `credential_object_valid.cbor`
- `credential_object_invalid.cbor`
- `credential_allow_allow.cbor`
- `credential_allow_deny.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
- MUST use structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
- MUST NOT log secret bytes; log only stable IDs (`SecretId`, `CredentialId`) and redacted metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test suite is deterministic and runs in CI.
- Denials produce DecisionReceipts with correct reason codes and evidence.
- Secret bytes never appear in logs, errors, or artifacts.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nnp3h","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: ObjectId::new keyed-hash collision-space + domain-separation fuzz","description":"fcp_core::ObjectId::new (object.rs:61-68) is the keyed-hash content-addressing
primitive: BLAKE3-keyed(b'FCP2-OBJECT-V2' || zone || schema.hash() || content).
Every security-object identity in the mesh derives from this function.

Existing fuzz coverage focuses on the WAL verifier (object_id_verifier.rs) and
on consumers that take an ObjectId by value (iblt, mesh_post_verify_*). The
constructor's domain-separation properties — i.e. the guarantees a regression
in the hash construction would silently violate — are NOT exercised:

  - **Content-binding**: bit-flip in content MUST change the ObjectId.
  - **Zone-binding**: same content under different zones MUST yield distinct
    ids. (Without this, an attacker who pivots a payload between zones could
    forge a stable cross-zone identity.)
  - **Schema-binding**: same content under different SchemaIds MUST yield
    distinct ids.
  - **Key-binding**: different ObjectIdKey on identical inputs MUST yield
    distinct ids — this is what enforces the per-zone privacy guarantee
    against dictionary attacks documented at object.rs:118-120.
  - **Domain separation from from_unscoped_bytes**: ObjectId::new(c,z,s,k) MUST
    NOT collide with from_unscoped_bytes(c) for any inputs (the unscoped form
    is documented NON-NORMATIVE; cross-collision would let an attacker promote
    an unscoped hash into a security-object identity).
  - **parse_prefixed round-trip**: id.to_prefixed_string() → parse_prefixed
    is identity for every constructed id.

A regression that dropped one of the four mix steps (the b'FCP2-OBJECT-V2'
domain tag, the zone, the schema hash, or the keyed-hash bind) would
collapse one of these bindings without breaking any existing test.

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/object_id_construction.rs
  - 6 properties + once-gated regression anchor for the
    ObjectId::new ≠ from_unscoped_bytes domain-separation property.
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
  - cargo +nightly check -p fcp-fuzz clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:14:25.208922Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:21:34.492760Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:21:34.492166Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3036,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nnp3h","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit 204c5113. fuzz/fuzz_targets/object_id_construction.rs — 8 properties + once-gated anchors covering keyed-hash domain separation, content/zone/schema/key bindings, parse_prefixed round-trip, and the SchemaId separator-aliasing regression class. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:21:34Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nuc","title":"fcp.twilio: Verify 2FA authentication service","description":"# fcp.twilio: Verify 2FA authentication service (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Twilio Verify to support 2FA/verification workflows:
- send verification codes
- check verification codes

This surface is security-sensitive and must be tightly capability scoped.

## Scope
- Create a verification (send code via SMS/call/etc).
- Check a verification (validate code).
- Optional: cancel verification.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.verify.send` (dangerous-ish: sends messages)
- `twilio.verify.check` (auth-sensitive)
- Optional: `twilio.verify.cancel`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - phone numbers are sensitive (never log)
  - verification codes are secrets (never log)
- For sends:
  - receipts/audit events include verification SID and destination hash only

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.verify.send`
  - `twilio.verify.check`
  - Optional: `twilio.verify.cancel`
- Strict validation:
  - code length/format
  - channel allowlist

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - send/check flows
  - redaction (no codes/numbers in logs)
  - error mapping (invalid code, expired, rate limited)

## Acceptance criteria
- Verify operations are mechanically authorized, safe, and do not leak secrets.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-rz5y1","title":"[REVIEW R2][HIGH] fcp-e2e: 14 compliance harnesses still mask connector handshake semantics (mechanical fix — apply c9561961 / d18041eb template)","description":"Round-2 fresh-eyes review of fcp-e2e/tests/*.rs second-half suites. Inventory confirms 14 compliance harnesses still carry the canonical pre-fix masked handshake pattern that was explicitly fixed for terraform (commit d18041eb) and spotify (commit c9561961):

Affected files (all under crates/fcp-e2e/tests/):
- mailchimp_compliance_e2e.rs:68-99
- metabase_compliance_e2e.rs (handshake fn)
- monday_compliance_e2e.rs
- n8n_compliance_e2e.rs
- onepassword_compliance_e2e.rs
- postgresql_compliance_e2e.rs
- retool_compliance_e2e.rs
- segment_compliance_e2e.rs
- sendgrid_compliance_e2e.rs:67-98
- snowflake_compliance_e2e.rs:68-99
- todoist_compliance_e2e.rs
- trello_compliance_e2e.rs
- webhook_receiver_compliance_e2e.rs:67-100
- zapier_compliance_e2e.rs

Masked pattern (canonical — sendgrid shown, all 14 are byte-equivalent modulo connector name):

    async fn handshake(&mut self, req: HandshakeRequest) -> FcpResult<HandshakeResponse> {
        self.connector
            .handle_handshake(json!({ "session_id": "<name>-e2e-session" }))
            .await?;
        self.verifier = Some(CapabilityVerifier::new(...));
        Ok(HandshakeResponse {
            status: "accepted".to_string(),
            capabilities_granted: req.capabilities_requested.iter().cloned()
                .map(|capability| CapabilityGrant { capability, operation: None }).collect(),
            session_id: SessionId::new(),
            manifest_hash: "sha256:<name>-e2e".to_string(),
            nonce: req.nonce,
            event_caps: None, auth_caps: None, op_catalog_hash: None,
        })
    }

Problems:
1. Hardcoded session_id string — does not match what adapter reports back to the host.
2. Connector response discarded entirely — no binding, no assertions.
3. capabilities_granted echoes req.capabilities_requested wholesale — no intersection with what the connector actually declares. If the connector drops a capability from its advertised list, the harness still grants it.
4. protocol_version, connector_id, capabilities-array drift on the connector side is invisible to the suite.

Fix — apply the c9561961 / d18041eb template verbatim to each of the 14:
- Generate SessionId::new() up front; inject into serialized req as params.session_id.
- Call self.connector.handle_handshake(params).await? and bind the response.
- Assert response.protocol_version == "2.0" (or the connector's declared version); diagnostic on drift.
- Assert response.connector_id is a string (presence check).
- Collect response.capabilities into BTreeSet<String>.
- capabilities_granted = req.capabilities_requested.iter().filter(|c| connector_caps.contains(c.as_str())).cloned().map(...).collect() — intersection only.
- HandshakeResponse uses the adapter-generated session_id (not fresh SessionId::new()).

Template commits: d18041eb (fcp-e2e/terraform), c9561961 (fcp-e2e/spotify). Each applies ~70 LOC per file. Mechanical; low-risk; can ship as a single batched commit or as 14 connector-scoped commits.

Related context:
- offline_repair_e2e.rs: N/A (no connector handshake; out of scope).
- s3_compliance_e2e.rs: architecturally different — the S3 connector emits a full HandshakeResponse shape and verifies capabilities internally (connector.rs:782). Adapter's serde_json::from_value(response) roundtrip and absent adapter-level invoke verify are consistent with that design. No fix needed here.
- spotify/terraform: already FIXED.
- CapabilityVerifier::verify owned-token fanout (t5453): uniformly applied across all 14 MASKED suites. No additional fanout work required.

Suggested severity: P1/HIGH. Same class of bug as the already-closed br-619oy (terraform) and br-9sztj / br-a6ouw (spotify). Filing as a single roll-up because the fix is mechanical — an IMPLEMENTER can rotate through all 14 with the template in ~4 hours.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T19:17:51.697069Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-24T20:13:53.225300Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T20:13:53.225051Z","close_reason":"Resolved by commits 9fbcbcb1 (cod_1 last 7 harnesses) + 130772a0 (cc_2 first 7 harnesses); all 14 masked-handshake suites now invoke real connector","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2847,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-rz5y1","author":"jemanuel","text":"First half (7 of 14 suites) shipped in commit 130772a0 by cc_2.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s4c4","title":"TEST: Telegram Connector Integration Tests","description":"# TEST: Telegram Connector Integration Tests (Deterministic)


## Goal
Validate Telegram connector integration flows (updates, sends, webhook/polling harness) with deterministic tests and mocks.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Purpose
Integration tests for `fcp.telegram` that validate **real connector behavior** against a **deterministic fake Telegram Bot API** (wiremock or a purpose-built in-process server).

These tests are NOT allowed to call the real Telegram API.

## What this test suite must prove
### 1) Bot API correctness (request/response)
- getUpdates long-poll semantics (timeout handling is bounded)
- update offset advancement and persistence
- sendMessage / sendMedia request formation
- file download URL handling (if supported)

### 2) Cursor + singleton-writer correctness
- only the lease-holder advances the update cursor
- restart behavior: resume from persisted cursor
- duplicate update defense (idempotent event emission)

### 3) FCP2 connector contract (integration-level)
- standard method surface works end-to-end (handshake/describe/introspect/capabilities/configure/invoke/health/shutdown)
- capability gating is mechanical:
  - no token → deny + DecisionReceipt
  - valid token → allow
- streaming/event emission:
  - events emitted as EventEnvelopes with correct topic
  - inbound data is tainted as external input

### 4) Error and adversity handling
- rate limits (429) and Retry-After
- invalid token (401) fail-closed
- network timeouts and bounded retries
- malformed update payloads (reject + audit, never panic)

## Required scenarios (minimum)
- scripted update stream: new message → edit → member join → member leave
- concurrent start attempt:
  - second instance denied/fenced by lease
- send operation with simulated timeout-after-send:
  - verify fail-closed behavior to avoid duplicate messages

## Logging requirements
- Structured JSON logs with: `test_name`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, `duration_ms`.
- For denials: `decision=deny`, `reason_code`, `evidence` (object IDs).
- Never log bot tokens or raw message bodies by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI.
- Produces a clear, searchable log trail on failure.
- Integrates cleanly with the shared E2E/compliance framework (no bespoke harness logic that can’t be reused).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-s5u65","title":"[PERF] fcp-store::MemorySymbolStore::get_symbol: read path takes global write lock","description":"crates/fcp-store/src/symbol_store.rs:392-455. get_symbol(), get_all_symbols(), and symbol_count() all acquire objects.write() just to scrub corrupt symbols and read one object's symbol map. Under concurrent request load that turns every symbol lookup into a serialized writer critical section, blocking unrelated readers and writers on the entire store. The hot-path cost is contention rather than raw CPU: aggregate throughput collapses as lookup concurrency rises even when no corruption is present. Proposed improvement: make the fast path read-only (objects.read()), move corruption scrubbing to a background or per-object maintenance path, or shard/per-object lock the symbol maps so a single lookup does not take the global write lock.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:27.974311Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:11:06.764103Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:11:06.763587Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2836,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-s5u65","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit 24745bb0.

Fix: read-first + write-on-corruption across all three read methods:
- get_symbol: objects.read() fast path for the healthy case; falls through to objects.write() ONLY when a mismatched symbol is found at the target ESI. Hot case never takes the write lock.
- get_all_symbols / symbol_count: pure read-lock paths that filter via symbol_matches_meta at read time instead of physically scrubbing the map. Observable output unchanged.

Physical pruning of corrupt entries defers to the next write op (put_symbol, put_object_meta, delete_object) which already hold the write lock. used_bytes may lag briefly on corrupt entries — acceptable given it's already eventually-consistent under concurrent puts.

Regression tests:
- get_symbol_read_path_is_concurrent_safe: 32 OS threads × (8 get_symbol + symbol_count) on the same object; asserts every reader agrees count == 8. Under the old code readers serialized via the global write lock.
- get_symbol_fast_path_errors_preserve_semantics: pins ObjectNotFound / NotFound error shapes so the read-lock contract can't silently regress.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-store — 805 pass (803 + 2 new), same 2 pre-existing flaky quarantine::evict_expired* failures.

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:11:06Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-xedeu","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: NodeKeyAttestation sign/verify field-binding + tag canonicalization fuzz","description":"fcp_tailscale::NodeKeyAttestation::sign + verify (identity.rs:302-387)
is the owner-signed attestation that binds (node_id, signing_kid,
encryption_kid, issuance_kid, tags, issued_at, expires_at) under an
Ed25519 signature. The mesh's identity model relies on this binding —
fcp_tags() and verified_fcp_tags() (identity.rs:227-246) refuse to
emit a node's zone memberships unless the attestation verifies, so any
binding-regression in sign/verify lets an attacker spoof zone tags or
swap a key for one they control.

Existing fuzz coverage in fcp-tailscale:
  - tailscale_acl: ZoneTagMapping zone↔tag round-trip + TailscaleTag::new
  - tailscale_status_parse: TailscaleStatus JSON deserialization
    + peers() consistency

NOT covered: the attestation sign/verify surface. Properties a regression
would silently break:

  1. **Round-trip**: sign(...) → verify(...) succeeds for the same
     (node_id, keys, tags, owner) tuple with sufficient validity_hours.
  2. **Tag canonicalization**: canonical_tag_strings (identity.rs:266-
     271) sort_unstable + dedup before signing — so signing with
     tags=[A,B,C] MUST verify under tags=[C,B,A] (sort) AND under
     tags=[A,A,B,C] (dedup). A regression that dropped the sort or
     dedup would let an attacker swap a captured attestation's tag
     ORDER (or insert duplicates) and pass through verify.
  3. **Node-id binding**: verify with a different node_id MUST yield
     InvalidAttestation — guards against pivoting a captured
     attestation across nodes.
  4. **Key-set binding (×3)**: verify with a NodeKeys whose
     signing_kid, encryption_kid, OR issuance_kid differs from the
     sign-time keys MUST yield InvalidAttestation. A regression that
     dropped any of the three from the canonical signing bytes lets
     an attacker substitute their own key for that role.
  5. **Tag-list binding**: verify with a tag set whose canonical sorted-
     dedup'd form differs from the signing-time canonical form MUST
     yield InvalidAttestation.
  6. **Owner-key binding**: verify with a different owner pubkey MUST
     yield InvalidAttestation (the signer_kid != owner.key_id() path
     at identity.rs:361 closes this BEFORE signature verification, and
     the signature path itself closes the case where the signer_kid
     happens to match).

Acceptance:
  - fuzz/fuzz_targets/tailscale_attestation_binding.rs
  - 6 MRs above + once-gated regression anchor for tag canonicalization
    (sort + dedup) using two hand-constructed tag-permutation pairs.
  - Registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml.
